title: More testosterone won’t make a better soldier or a tougher man – aggression and strength drive T levels, not the other way around
excerpt: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is requiring service members to screen for low testosterone levels. Ironically, the military lifestyle itself is more likely to lower T levels.
content_html:
How low is too low when it comes to testosterone levels? When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced in July 2026 that U.S. service members ages 30 and older will have their testosterone tested every year, neither he nor the Pentagon specified what level of the hormone would be considered “low” enough to require treatment – a telling omission, since where “low” begins depends on the lab conducting the test and the guidelines your doctor follows.

The announcement lands amid a national testosterone craze: Prescriptions have climbed to nearly 12 million in 2025, up 154% since 2020, and fastest among men in their late 30s and 40s. Online clinics that sell testosterone as a lifestyle upgrade have largely driven this rise in prescriptions.

As a psychologist who studies masculinity, I’m certain that while the idea that more testosterone makes a man tougher and more fit to fight seems intuitive, it’s also mostly wrong.

What testosterone actually does

Testosterone is often considered a “male” hormone, but people of every gender produce it in differing amounts.

In cisgender boys and men, testosterone levels rise at puberty, helping build muscle, grow body hair and produce sperm. Levels naturally decline in adulthood, typically beginning age 30 and onward. When levels drop significantly below normal and a person begins to experience symptoms such as extreme fatigue, low libido or drastic muscle loss, testosterone replacement therapy with medical supervision can help.

But “normal” is a vast range. Endocrinologists consider testosterone levels between roughly 300 to 1,000 nanograms per deciliter as healthy. Within that range, a man’s testosterone level tells you little about the “masculine” traits that some people think it governs.

Testosterone is tied to muscle mass, but in healthy men, it doesn’t track with actual strength – training, sleep and nutrition matter far more. As endocrinologist Adrian Dobs put it, a person at 700 isn’t stronger or more of a man than one at 300.

Moreover, an individual’s testosterone levels aren’t fixed – they swing by the hour and the season. So doctors usually test testosterone levels only when symptoms appear. Screening healthy men by the millions may be potentially harmful.

For instance, testosterone therapy often carries risks. Supplementing the hormone signals the body to shut down production of its own supply, which can shrink the testicles and suppress sperm. Some researchers have even studied testosterone for its contraceptive effects.

Moreover, because testosterone revs up oil glands and hair follicles – and partly converts them to estrogen – it can also bring about acne, balding and even some breast tissue growth.

This “high-T” ideal – sold by online clinics and now endorsed from the top – glosses over these severe risks.

The macho myth

Decades of research undercut Hegseth’s core claim that more testosterone means a more aggressive, dominant, masculine man.

A 2001 analysis of data from 45 studies covering nearly 10,000 people found only a small correlation between testosterone levels and aggression. When researchers pooled data in 2020 from experiments that actually administered testosterone to participants to study whether it causes aggression, they found only weak evidence. In fact, they saw that the effects of testosterone on aggression primarily showed up in men who were more dominant or impulsive.

If anything, the arrow runs the opposite way – behavior drives testosterone levels. One study found that men who handled a gun for 15 minutes had bigger testosterone spikes and acted more aggressively than men who touched a board game. Meanwhile, the testosterone levels of new fathers fell more steeply the more they physically cared for their child.

When men do demonstrate aggression, the trigger is typically social. In my own research, threatening a man’s sense of manhood – but not a woman’s womanhood – reliably provokes aggression, and the men who react most strongly are those who feel the most pressure to live up to masculine ideals.

The link between aggression and the fear of failing at manhood takes root early. In a study of more than 200 adolescent boys and their parents, my team and I found that boys who reacted aggressively to threats to their masculinity were mainly those who felt external pressure to be masculine. That pressure was strongest among boys whose parents endorsed specific beliefs about manhood, like the idea that men should hold power over others.

Biology, social pressure and development work together to determine how a boy grows into a man. Unlike Hegseth’s story that manhood is a chemical to be topped off, research shows that while hormones may open a window, the social environment decides what comes through.

Military life lowers testosterone

Ironically, the grind of military service can drive down a man’s testosterone levels.

A 2020 study of U.S. Army Rangers found that the sleep loss built into training significantly reduced testosterone levels.

Chronic stress, sleep deprivation, blast exposure and poor nutrition can produce a cluster of health problems called “Operator Syndrome.” In some cases, the work conditions of special operators can push a 35-year-old’s testosterone levels toward those typical of a man in his 80s.

Notice the loop: The military imposes the very conditions that suppress testosterone, then proposes to fix the result with more of it. The psychologist who named Operator Syndrome cautioned that handing troops testosterone won’t restore their health, because the real culprits are sleep, stress and injury.

Double standards

In February 2025, the Pentagon halted gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender troops, framing such care as incompatible with the force’s “readiness” and “lethality.” Yet prescribing testosterone to bring a cisgender man’s body in line with the Pentagon’s masculine ideals is hormone therapy, too.

Legal advocates flagged the contradiction at once: The same class of medication is disqualifying for one group of service members, but for another, it’s rebranded as optimizing the soldier.

So a testosterone mandate won’t build tougher soldiers – if anything, it reveals an anxiety no hormone can fix. My research argues that the men most pressured to prove their manhood are the ones who most experience it as fragile, treating masculinity as a status that can be lost and must be won back again and again.

Telling troops that their readiness hinges on a test result can deepen their anxiety around manhood. Real strength – the kind that holds up under fire – is not something that can be topped off with a hormone. Although testosterone is biologically useful, it isn’t the measure of a man or soldier.

blocks.html_en: How low is too low when it comes to testosterone levels? When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced in July 2026 that U.S. service members ages 30 and older will have their testosterone tested every year, neither he nor the Pentagon specified what level of the hormone would be considered “low” enough to require treatment – a telling omission, since where “low” begins depends on the lab conducting the test and the guidelines your doctor follows.

sentences.en: How low is too low when it comes to testosterone levels?
sentences.zh: 睾酮水平低到什么程度才算太低？

sentences.en: When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced in July 2026 that U.S. service members ages 30 and older will have their testosterone tested every year, neither he nor the Pentagon specified what level of the hormone would be considered “low” enough to require treatment – a telling omission, since where “low” begins depends on the lab conducting the test and the guidelines your doctor follows.
sentences.zh: 当国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯 (Pete Hegseth) 于 2026 年 7 月宣布，30 岁及以上的美国军人将每年接受睾丸激素检测时，他和五角大楼都没有具体说明什么水平的激素会被认为“低”到需要治疗的程度——这是一个明显的遗漏，因为“低”的开始取决于进行检测的实验室和医生遵循的指南。

blocks.html_en: The announcement lands amid a national testosterone craze: Prescriptions have climbed to nearly 12 million in 2025, up 154% since 2020, and fastest among men in their late 30s and 40s. Online clinics that sell testosterone as a lifestyle upgrade have largely driven this rise in prescriptions.

sentences.en: The announcement lands amid a national testosterone craze: Prescriptions have climbed to nearly 12 million in 2025, up 154% since 2020, and fastest among men in their late 30s and 40s.
sentences.zh: 这一消息发布之际，正值全国睾丸激素热潮之际：2025 年，处方药数量已攀升至近 1200 万份，自 2020 年以来增长了 154%，增长最快的是 30 多岁和 40 多岁的男性。

sentences.en: Online clinics that sell testosterone as a lifestyle upgrade have largely driven this rise in prescriptions.
sentences.zh: 销售睾酮作为生活方式升级的在线诊所在很大程度上推动了处方数量的增加。

blocks.html_en: As a psychologist who studies masculinity, I’m certain that while the idea that more testosterone makes a man tougher and more fit to fight seems intuitive, it’s also mostly wrong.

sentences.en: As a psychologist who studies masculinity, I’m certain that while the idea that more testosterone makes a man tougher and more fit to fight seems intuitive, it’s also mostly wrong.
sentences.zh: 作为一名研究男性气质的心理学家，我确信，虽然更多的睾丸激素使男人更坚强、更适合战斗的想法似乎很直观，但它也大多是错误的。

sentences.en: Medical guidelines recommend against blanket testosterone testing.
sentences.zh: 医学指南建议不要进行全面睾酮测试。

blocks.html_en: What testosterone actually does

sentences.en: What testosterone actually does
sentences.zh: 睾酮的实际作用是什么

blocks.html_en: Testosterone is often considered a “male” hormone, but people of every gender produce it in differing amounts.

sentences.en: Testosterone is often considered a “male” hormone, but people of every gender produce it in differing amounts.
sentences.zh: 睾酮通常被认为是一种“男性”激素，但不同性别的人产生的睾酮量不同。

blocks.html_en: In cisgender boys and men, testosterone levels rise at puberty, helping build muscle, grow body hair and produce sperm. Levels naturally decline in adulthood, typically beginning age 30 and onward. When levels drop significantly below normal and a person begins to experience symptoms such as extreme fatigue, low libido or drastic muscle loss, testosterone replacement therapy with medical supervision can help.

sentences.en: In cisgender boys and men, testosterone levels rise at puberty, helping build muscle, grow body hair and produce sperm.
sentences.zh: 在顺性别男孩和男性中，睾丸激素水平在青春期上升，有助于增强肌肉、生长体毛和产生精子。

sentences.en: Levels naturally decline in adulthood, typically beginning age 30 and onward.
sentences.zh: 成年后，通常从 30 岁起，水平自然下降。

sentences.en: When levels drop significantly below normal and a person begins to experience symptoms such as extreme fatigue, low libido or drastic muscle loss, testosterone replacement therapy with medical supervision can help.
sentences.zh: 当水平明显低于正常水平并且一个人开始出现极度疲劳、性欲低下或肌肉严重丧失等症状时，在医疗监督下的睾酮替代疗法可能会有所帮助。

blocks.html_en: But “normal” is a vast range. Endocrinologists consider testosterone levels between roughly 300 to 1,000 nanograms per deciliter as healthy. Within that range, a man’s testosterone level tells you little about the “masculine” traits that some people think it governs.

sentences.en: But “normal” is a vast range.
sentences.zh: 但“正常”的范围很广。

sentences.en: Endocrinologists consider testosterone levels between roughly 300 to 1,000 nanograms per deciliter as healthy.
sentences.zh: 内分泌学家认为睾酮水平在大约每分升 300 至 1,000 纳克之间是健康的。

sentences.en: Within that range, a man’s testosterone level tells you little about the “masculine” traits that some people think it governs.
sentences.zh: 在这个范围内，男性的睾酮水平几乎无法告诉你一些人认为它控制的“男性”特征。

blocks.html_en: Testosterone is tied to muscle mass, but in healthy men, it doesn’t track with actual strength – training, sleep and nutrition matter far more. As endocrinologist Adrian Dobs put it, a person at 700 isn’t stronger or more of a man than one at 300.

sentences.en: Testosterone is tied to muscle mass, but in healthy men, it doesn’t track with actual strength – training, sleep and nutrition matter far more.
sentences.zh: 睾丸激素与肌肉质量有关，但对于健康男性来说，它并不与实际力量相匹配——训练、睡眠和营养更重要。

sentences.en: As endocrinologist Adrian Dobs put it, a person at 700 isn’t stronger or more of a man than one at 300.
sentences.zh: 正如内分泌学家阿德里安·多布斯所说，700 岁的人并不比 300 岁的人更强壮或更有男子气概。

blocks.html_en: Moreover, an individual’s testosterone levels aren’t fixed – they swing by the hour and the season. So doctors usually test testosterone levels only when symptoms appear. Screening healthy men by the millions may be potentially harmful.

sentences.en: Moreover, an individual’s testosterone levels aren’t fixed – they swing by the hour and the season.
sentences.zh: 此外，一个人的睾酮水平并不是固定的——它们会随着时间和季节的变化而变化。

sentences.en: So doctors usually test testosterone levels only when symptoms appear.
sentences.zh: 因此，医生通常仅在症状出现时才测试睾酮水平。

sentences.en: Screening healthy men by the millions may be potentially harmful.
sentences.zh: 对数以百万计的健康男性进行筛查可能具有潜在的危害。

sentences.en: The body converts excess testosterone to estrogen.
sentences.zh: 身体将多余的睾酮转化为雌激素。

sentences.en: Boghog2/Wikimedia Commons
sentences.zh: Boghog2/维基共享资源

blocks.html_en: For instance, testosterone therapy often carries risks. Supplementing the hormone signals the body to shut down production of its own supply, which can shrink the testicles and suppress sperm. Some researchers have even studied testosterone for its contraceptive effects.

sentences.en: For instance, testosterone therapy often carries risks.
sentences.zh: 例如，睾酮疗法通常会带来风险。

sentences.en: Supplementing the hormone signals the body to shut down production of its own supply, which can shrink the testicles and suppress sperm.
sentences.zh: 补充这种激素会向身体发出信号，停止自身供应，从而缩小睾丸并抑制精子。

sentences.en: Some researchers have even studied testosterone for its contraceptive effects.
sentences.zh: 一些研究人员甚至研究了睾酮的避孕作用。

blocks.html_en: Moreover, because testosterone revs up oil glands and hair follicles – and partly converts them to estrogen – it can also bring about acne, balding and even some breast tissue growth.

sentences.en: Moreover, because testosterone revs up oil glands and hair follicles – and partly converts them to estrogen – it can also bring about acne, balding and even some breast tissue growth.
sentences.zh: 此外，由于睾酮会加速皮脂腺和毛囊的生长，并将其部分转化为雌激素，因此它还会导致痤疮、秃顶，甚至一些乳房组织生长。

blocks.html_en: This “high-T” ideal – sold by online clinics and now endorsed from the top – glosses over these severe risks.

sentences.en: This “high-T” ideal – sold by online clinics and now endorsed from the top – glosses over these severe risks.
sentences.zh: 这种“高T”理想——由在线诊所出售，现在得到了高层的认可——掩盖了这些严重的风险。

blocks.html_en: The macho myth

sentences.en: The macho myth
sentences.zh: 男子气概的神话

blocks.html_en: Decades of research undercut Hegseth’s core claim that more testosterone means a more aggressive, dominant, masculine man.

sentences.en: Decades of research undercut Hegseth’s core claim that more testosterone means a more aggressive, dominant, masculine man.
sentences.zh: 数十年的研究削弱了赫格斯的核心主张，即更多的睾丸激素意味着一个更具攻击性、更具统治力、更有男子气概的男人。

blocks.html_en: A 2001 analysis of data from 45 studies covering nearly 10,000 people found only a small correlation between testosterone levels and aggression. When researchers pooled data in 2020 from experiments that actually administered testosterone to participants to study whether it causes aggression, they found only weak evidence. In fact, they saw that the effects of testosterone on aggression primarily showed up in men who were more dominant or impulsive.

sentences.en: A 2001 analysis of data from 45 studies covering nearly 10,000 people found only a small correlation between testosterone levels and aggression.
sentences.zh: 2001 年，对涵盖近 10,000 人的 45 项研究的数据进行的分析发现，睾酮水平与攻击性之间只有很小的相关性。

sentences.en: When researchers pooled data in 2020 from experiments that actually administered testosterone to participants to study whether it causes aggression, they found only weak evidence.
sentences.zh: 2020 年，当研究人员汇集了实际向参与者注射睾酮的实验数据来研究它是否会导致攻击性时，他们只发现了微弱的证据。

sentences.en: In fact, they saw that the effects of testosterone on aggression primarily showed up in men who were more dominant or impulsive.
sentences.zh: 事实上，他们发现睾丸激素对攻击性的影响主要表现在更具统治力或冲动的男性身上。

sentences.en: Boys who feel pressure to be manly learn to react aggressively to perceived threats to their masculinity.
sentences.zh: 那些感到有男子气概压力的男孩会学会对自己的男子气概受到威胁时做出积极的反应。

sentences.en: Jeff Gritchen/Digital First Media/Orange County Register via Getty Images

blocks.html_en: If anything, the arrow runs the opposite way – behavior drives testosterone levels. One study found that men who handled a gun for 15 minutes had bigger testosterone spikes and acted more aggressively than men who touched a board game. Meanwhile, the testosterone levels of new fathers fell more steeply the more they physically cared for their child.

sentences.en: If anything, the arrow runs the opposite way – behavior drives testosterone levels.
sentences.zh: 如果说有什么不同的话，那就是箭头的方向相反——行为会驱动睾酮水平。

sentences.en: One study found that men who handled a gun for 15 minutes had bigger testosterone spikes and acted more aggressively than men who touched a board game.
sentences.zh: 一项研究发现，持枪 15 分钟的男性比接触棋盘游戏的男性睾丸激素峰值更高，而且表现得更具攻击性。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, the testosterone levels of new fathers fell more steeply the more they physically cared for their child.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，新爸爸们对孩子的照顾越多，睾丸激素水平下降得越厉害。

blocks.html_en: When men do demonstrate aggression, the trigger is typically social. In my own research, threatening a man’s sense of manhood – but not a woman’s womanhood – reliably provokes aggression, and the men who react most strongly are those who feel the most pressure to live up to masculine ideals.

sentences.en: When men do demonstrate aggression, the trigger is typically social.
sentences.zh: 当男性确实表现出攻击性时，触发因素通常是社交。

sentences.en: In my own research, threatening a man’s sense of manhood – but not a woman’s womanhood – reliably provokes aggression, and the men who react most strongly are those who feel the most pressure to live up to masculine ideals.
sentences.zh: 在我自己的研究中，威胁男性的男子气概——而不是女性的女性气质——确实会引发攻击性，而反应最强烈的男性是那些感受到实现男性理想的压力最大的人。

blocks.html_en: The link between aggression and the fear of failing at manhood takes root early. In a study of more than 200 adolescent boys and their parents, my team and I found that boys who reacted aggressively to threats to their masculinity were mainly those who felt external pressure to be masculine. That pressure was strongest among boys whose parents endorsed specific beliefs about manhood, like the idea that men should hold power over others.

sentences.en: The link between aggression and the fear of failing at manhood takes root early.
sentences.zh: 攻击性和对成年后失败的恐惧之间的联系很早就扎根了。

sentences.en: In a study of more than 200 adolescent boys and their parents, my team and I found that boys who reacted aggressively to threats to their masculinity were mainly those who felt external pressure to be masculine.
sentences.zh: 在一项针对 200 多名青春期男孩及其父母的研究中，我和我的团队发现，对男性气概的威胁做出攻击性反应的男孩主要是那些感受到外部压力、要求表现出男性气概的男孩。

sentences.en: That pressure was strongest among boys whose parents endorsed specific beliefs about manhood, like the idea that men should hold power over others.
sentences.zh: 这种压力在父母认可有关男子气概的特定信念的男孩中最为强烈，例如男性应该拥有凌驾于他人之上的权力。

blocks.html_en: Biology, social pressure and development work together to determine how a boy grows into a man. Unlike Hegseth’s story that manhood is a chemical to be topped off, research shows that while hormones may open a window, the social environment decides what comes through.

sentences.en: Biology, social pressure and development work together to determine how a boy grows into a man.
sentences.zh: 生物学、社会压力和发育共同决定男孩如何成长为男人。

sentences.en: Unlike Hegseth’s story that manhood is a chemical to be topped off, research shows that while hormones may open a window, the social environment decides what comes through.
sentences.zh: 与赫格斯的故事不同，即男子气概是一种需要补充的化学物质，研究表明，虽然荷尔蒙可能会打开一扇窗，但社会环境决定了会发生什么。

blocks.html_en: Military life lowers testosterone

sentences.en: Military life lowers testosterone
sentences.zh: 军事生活会降低睾酮水平

blocks.html_en: Ironically, the grind of military service can drive down a man’s testosterone levels.

sentences.en: Ironically, the grind of military service can drive down a man’s testosterone levels.
sentences.zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，服兵役的辛苦会降低男性的睾丸激素水平。

blocks.html_en: A 2020 study of U.S. Army Rangers found that the sleep loss built into training significantly reduced testosterone levels.

sentences.en: A 2020 study of U.S. Army Rangers found that the sleep loss built into training significantly reduced testosterone levels.
sentences.zh: 2020 年对美国陆军游骑兵的一项研究发现，训练中的睡眠不足会显着降低睾酮水平。

blocks.html_en: Chronic stress, sleep deprivation, blast exposure and poor nutrition can produce a cluster of health problems called “Operator Syndrome.” In some cases, the work conditions of special operators can push a 35-year-old’s testosterone levels toward those typical of a man in his 80s.

sentences.en: Chronic stress, sleep deprivation, blast exposure and poor nutrition can produce a cluster of health problems called “Operator Syndrome.”
sentences.zh: 慢性压力、睡眠不足、爆炸暴露和营养不良会产生一系列健康问题，称为“操作员综合症”。

sentences.en: In some cases, the work conditions of special operators can push a 35-year-old’s testosterone levels toward those typical of a man in his 80s.
sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，特种操作员的工作条件会使 35 岁的男性睾丸激素水平接近 80 多岁男性的典型水平。

sentences.en: The stress of military life lowers testosterone levels.
sentences.zh: 军事生活的压力会降低睾酮水平。

sentences.en: Allison Joyce/AFP via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 艾莉森·乔伊斯/法新社，盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: Notice the loop: The military imposes the very conditions that suppress testosterone, then proposes to fix the result with more of it. The psychologist who named Operator Syndrome cautioned that handing troops testosterone won’t restore their health, because the real culprits are sleep, stress and injury.

sentences.en: Notice the loop: The military imposes the very conditions that suppress testosterone, then proposes to fix the result with more of it.
sentences.zh: 注意这个循环：军方强加了抑制睾丸激素的条件，然后提议用更多的睾丸激素来修复结果。

sentences.en: The psychologist who named Operator Syndrome cautioned that handing troops testosterone won’t restore their health, because the real culprits are sleep, stress and injury.
sentences.zh: 被命名为“操作员综合症”的心理学家警告说，向士兵提供睾丸激素并不能恢复他们的健康，因为真正的罪魁祸首是睡眠、压力和伤害。

blocks.html_en: Double standards

sentences.en: Double standards
sentences.zh: 双重标准

blocks.html_en: In February 2025, the Pentagon halted gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender troops, framing such care as incompatible with the force’s “readiness” and “lethality.” Yet prescribing testosterone to bring a cisgender man’s body in line with the Pentagon’s masculine ideals is hormone therapy, too.

sentences.en: In February 2025, the Pentagon halted gender-affirming hormone therapy for transgender troops, framing such care as incompatible with the force’s “readiness” and “lethality.”
sentences.zh: 2025 年 2 月，五角大楼停止对跨性别部队进行性别确认激素治疗，认为此类护理与部队的“战备状态”和“杀伤力”不相容。

sentences.en: Yet prescribing testosterone to bring a cisgender man’s body in line with the Pentagon’s masculine ideals is hormone therapy, too.
sentences.zh: 然而，通过服用睾丸激素来使顺性别男性的身体符合五角大楼的男性理想也属于激素疗法。

blocks.html_en: Legal advocates flagged the contradiction at once: The same class of medication is disqualifying for one group of service members, but for another, it’s rebranded as optimizing the soldier.

sentences.en: Legal advocates flagged the contradiction at once: The same class of medication is disqualifying for one group of service members, but for another, it’s rebranded as optimizing the soldier.
sentences.zh: 法律倡导者立即指出了这一矛盾：同一类别的药物对于一组服役人员来说是取消资格的，但对于另一组服役人员来说，它被重新命名为优化士兵。

blocks.html_en: So a testosterone mandate won’t build tougher soldiers – if anything, it reveals an anxiety no hormone can fix. My research argues that the men most pressured to prove their manhood are the ones who most experience it as fragile, treating masculinity as a status that can be lost and must be won back again and again.

sentences.en: So a testosterone mandate won’t build tougher soldiers – if anything, it reveals an anxiety no hormone can fix.
sentences.zh: 因此，强制睾丸激素不会培养出更坚强的士兵——如果有的话，它揭示了一种激素无法解决的焦虑。

sentences.en: My research argues that the men most pressured to prove their manhood are the ones who most experience it as fragile, treating masculinity as a status that can be lost and must be won back again and again.
sentences.zh: 我的研究认为，那些最有压力证明自己的男子气概的男人是那些最脆弱的人，他们将男子气概视为一种可能会失去、必须一次又一次赢回来的地位。

blocks.html_en: Telling troops that their readiness hinges on a test result can deepen their anxiety around manhood. Real strength – the kind that holds up under fire – is not something that can be topped off with a hormone. Although testosterone is biologically useful, it isn’t the measure of a man or soldier.

sentences.en: Telling troops that their readiness hinges on a test result can deepen their anxiety around manhood.
sentences.zh: 告诉部队他们的准备情况取决于测试结果，可能会加深他们对男子气概的焦虑。

sentences.en: Real strength – the kind that holds up under fire – is not something that can be topped off with a hormone.
sentences.zh: 真正的力量——那种在火力下仍能抵抗的力量——不是可以用荷尔蒙来补充的。

sentences.en: Although testosterone is biologically useful, it isn’t the measure of a man or soldier.
sentences.zh: 尽管睾酮在生物学上是有用的，但它并不是衡量一个人或士兵的标准。

content_html_zh:
睾酮水平低到什么程度才算太低？ 当国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯 (Pete Hegseth) 于 2026 年 7 月宣布，30 岁及以上的美国军人将每年接受睾丸激素检测时，他和五角大楼都没有具体说明什么水平的激素会被认为“低”到需要治疗的程度——这是一个明显的遗漏，因为“低”的开始取决于进行检测的实验室和医生遵循的指南。

这一消息发布之际，正值全国睾丸激素热潮之际：2025 年，处方药数量已攀升至近 1200 万份，自 2020 年以来增长了 154%，增长最快的是 30 多岁和 40 多岁的男性。 销售睾酮作为生活方式升级的在线诊所在很大程度上推动了处方数量的增加。

作为一名研究男性气质的心理学家，我确信，虽然更多的睾丸激素使男人更坚强、更适合战斗的想法似乎很直观，但它也大多是错误的。

睾酮的实际作用是什么

睾酮通常被认为是一种“男性”激素，但不同性别的人产生的睾酮量不同。

在顺性别男孩和男性中，睾丸激素水平在青春期上升，有助于增强肌肉、生长体毛和产生精子。 成年后，通常从 30 岁起，水平自然下降。 当水平明显低于正常水平并且一个人开始出现极度疲劳、性欲低下或肌肉严重丧失等症状时，在医疗监督下的睾酮替代疗法可能会有所帮助。

但“正常”的范围很广。 内分泌学家认为睾酮水平在大约每分升 300 至 1,000 纳克之间是健康的。 在这个范围内，男性的睾酮水平几乎无法告诉你一些人认为它控制的“男性”特征。

睾丸激素与肌肉质量有关，但对于健康男性来说，它并不与实际力量相匹配——训练、睡眠和营养更重要。 正如内分泌学家阿德里安·多布斯所说，700 岁的人并不比 300 岁的人更强壮或更有男子气概。

此外，一个人的睾酮水平并不是固定的——它们会随着时间和季节的变化而变化。 因此，医生通常仅在症状出现时才测试睾酮水平。 对数以百万计的健康男性进行筛查可能具有潜在的危害。

例如，睾酮疗法通常会带来风险。 补充这种激素会向身体发出信号，停止自身供应，从而缩小睾丸并抑制精子。 一些研究人员甚至研究了睾酮的避孕作用。

此外，由于睾酮会加速皮脂腺和毛囊的生长，并将其部分转化为雌激素，因此它还会导致痤疮、秃顶，甚至一些乳房组织生长。

这种“高T”理想——由在线诊所出售，现在得到了高层的认可——掩盖了这些严重的风险。

男子气概的神话

数十年的研究削弱了赫格斯的核心主张，即更多的睾丸激素意味着一个更具攻击性、更具统治力、更有男子气概的男人。

2001 年，对涵盖近 10,000 人的 45 项研究的数据进行的分析发现，睾酮水平与攻击性之间只有很小的相关性。 2020 年，当研究人员汇集了实际向参与者注射睾酮的实验数据来研究它是否会导致攻击性时，他们只发现了微弱的证据。 事实上，他们发现睾丸激素对攻击性的影响主要表现在更具统治力或冲动的男性身上。

如果说有什么不同的话，那就是箭头的方向相反——行为会驱动睾酮水平。 一项研究发现，持枪 15 分钟的男性比接触棋盘游戏的男性睾丸激素峰值更高，而且表现得更具攻击性。 与此同时，新爸爸们对孩子的照顾越多，睾丸激素水平下降得越厉害。

当男性确实表现出攻击性时，触发因素通常是社交。 在我自己的研究中，威胁男性的男子气概——而不是女性的女性气质——确实会引发攻击性，而反应最强烈的男性是那些感受到实现男性理想的压力最大的人。

攻击性和对成年后失败的恐惧之间的联系很早就扎根了。 在一项针对 200 多名青春期男孩及其父母的研究中，我和我的团队发现，对男性气概的威胁做出攻击性反应的男孩主要是那些感受到外部压力、要求表现出男性气概的男孩。 这种压力在父母认可有关男子气概的特定信念的男孩中最为强烈，例如男性应该拥有凌驾于他人之上的权力。

生物学、社会压力和发育共同决定男孩如何成长为男人。 与赫格斯的故事不同，即男子气概是一种需要补充的化学物质，研究表明，虽然荷尔蒙可能会打开一扇窗，但社会环境决定了会发生什么。

军事生活会降低睾酮水平

具有讽刺意味的是，服兵役的辛苦会降低男性的睾丸激素水平。

2020 年对美国陆军游骑兵的一项研究发现，训练中的睡眠不足会显着降低睾酮水平。

慢性压力、睡眠不足、爆炸暴露和营养不良会产生一系列健康问题，称为“操作员综合症”。 在某些情况下，特种操作员的工作条件会使 35 岁的男性睾丸激素水平接近 80 多岁男性的典型水平。

注意这个循环：军方强加了抑制睾丸激素的条件，然后提议用更多的睾丸激素来修复结果。 被命名为“操作员综合症”的心理学家警告说，向士兵提供睾丸激素并不能恢复他们的健康，因为真正的罪魁祸首是睡眠、压力和伤害。

双重标准

2025 年 2 月，五角大楼停止对跨性别部队进行性别确认激素治疗，认为此类护理与部队的“战备状态”和“杀伤力”不相容。 然而，通过服用睾丸激素来使顺性别男性的身体符合五角大楼的男性理想也属于激素疗法。

法律倡导者立即指出了这一矛盾：同一类别的药物对于一组服役人员来说是取消资格的，但对于另一组服役人员来说，它被重新命名为优化士兵。

因此，强制睾丸激素不会培养出更坚强的士兵——如果有的话，它揭示了一种激素无法解决的焦虑。 我的研究认为，那些最有压力证明自己的男子气概的男人是那些最脆弱的人，他们将男子气概视为一种可能会失去、必须一次又一次赢回来的地位。

告诉部队他们的准备情况取决于测试结果，可能会加深他们对男子气概的焦虑。 真正的力量——那种在火力下仍能抵抗的力量——不是可以用荷尔蒙来补充的。 尽管睾酮在生物学上是有用的，但它并不是衡量一个人或士兵的标准。
